name: Lying Neck Curl (Flexion)
resistance_profile: Loads cervical flexion; use very light load and a slow tempo.
rom_notes: Lower the head to a gentle stretch, curl the chin toward the chest.
cues: Very light load, slow controlled tempo
Support the head with a towel/plate
execution_steps: Lie face-up on a bench with your head and neck extending just past the edge.
Rest a light plate wrapped in a towel on your forehead and hold it lightly in place.
Lower your head back under control to a gentle, pain-free stretch.
Curl your chin toward your chest, raising the plate smoothly with your neck flexors.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower slowly to the start and repeat.
good_when: Building neck size and flexion strength for contact or combat sports.
Balancing out posterior neck (extension) work for even development.
You want direct sagittal-plane neck training with adjustable load.
bad_when: You have any cervical pain, disc issue, or recent neck injury.
You are a total beginner training unsupervised with no coaching on tempo/load.
Headaches, dizziness, or nausea show up with loaded neck flexion.
common_errors: Using heavy load or jerky reps
